title: Projectile Motion Demystified
summary: Explore how objects move through the air after being launched without propulsion. Learn about the forces at play and discover why projectiles follow a curved path.

sections.heading: The Basics of Projectile Motion
sections.body: Projectile motion occurs when an object is thrown or propelled and moves under the influence of gravity alone. The path it takes is called a trajectory.
key_points: Launch without continuous force after launching
Object's curved flight due to Earth’s gravitational pull

sections.heading: Components Influencing Motion
sections.body: Three critical components dictate projectile motion: initial velocity, angle of projection relative to the ground, and gravity's acceleration. These influence both speed and direction.

sections.heading: Real-Life Application
sections.body: Understanding projectile motion is essential in fields such as ballistics, sports science, and even entertainment with fireworks. It's also a classic physics problem that helps students grasp the concepts of kinematics.

key_facts: Trajectory is parabolic due to gravity
